label: experimental/frontier
description: Why does separation preserve prosody but inject text hallucination at low overlap? Tests P2 (dimensionality effect) and P3 (pre-decode predictability) via reanalysis of findings #14, #21, #18.

dimensionality_table.dim_note: Whisper-zh vocab ~50k tokens; full sequence is high-dim discrete
1 bit: CER < 1.0 (transcript has some correct content)
token count / log-length; proxy = compression_ratio in #21

utterance_length_bound.interpretation: In the catastrophic hallucination regime, utterance length (1-dim text feature proxied by compression_ratio) is NOT preserved (mean CR ~18x = massive length inflation). This BOUNDS P2: dimensionality protects low-dim features in the moderate low-overlap tax regime, but the extreme confident-attractor collapses even the 1-dim length feature.

p2_dimensionality.evidence: At alpha=0.15 low/mid overlap, separation hurts high-dim text (mean CER benefit -1.207 < 0) but preserves low-dim features: speaker count (frac 1.00, always 2), binary transcript usability (frac 0.64), emotion prosody (frac 0.93). This supports P2 in the moderate tax regime. However #21 shows that in the catastrophic confident-attractor regime, even the 1-dim utterance-length feature is NOT preserved (mean compression_ratio 17.9x), so P2 is bounded: dimensionality explains the moderate asymmetry but not the extreme hallucination collapse.

p3_pre_decode_predictability.pre_decode_feature_note: overlap_ratio is an input property (truly pre-decode). no_speech_prob is Whisper's encoder-side no-speech token probability (max over segments; computed from encoder output at each segment start). Raw encoder embeddings are NOT stored in probe_rows.csv, so these are the best available pre-decode proxies.
p3_pre_decode_predictability.evidence: Best pre-decode LOO classifier AUC = 0.623 (threshold 0.6, direction learned in-fold = proper out-of-sample test). Single-feature LOO: overlap_ratio 0.000, no_speech_prob 0.623; combined overlap+nsp 0.588. For reference, the in-sample ranking AUC with oracle direction is 0.666 (no_speech_prob flipped 0.666) — this is an optimistic ceiling, not the verdict metric. The proper out-of-sample LOO AUC is above the 0.6 threshold. By contrast, post-decode compression_ratio LOO AUC = 0.846 (ranking 0.996) and the full post-decode LOO AUC = 0.992. The attractor is far more detectable post-decode than predictable pre-decode from the encoder-side signals stored in #21's data.

mechanism_synthesis: P2 supported + P3 weakly supported (best pre-decode LOO AUC 0.623) yields a two-layer mechanism. LAYER 1 (dimensionality, the dominant explanation): in the moderate low-overlap tax regime, separation artifacts are additive perturbations that a low-dimensional continuous estimator (prosody ~3 dims) averages out, but a high-dimensional discrete decoder (text ~50k tokens) cannot — the same artifact that is noise to prosody is confusion at the token-decision boundary. This explains the #14 asymmetry: emotion benefit >= 0 while CER benefit < 0 at low/mid overlap, and why low-dim text-derived features (speaker count, binary usability) are also preserved there. LAYER 2 (pre-decode predictability): no_speech_prob (Whisper's encoder-side no-speech token probability) carries a WEAK pre-decode signal (LOO AUC 0.623, just above the 0.6 threshold; in-sample oracle-direction ceiling 0.666). The attractor onset is marginally predictable pre-decode but far more reliably detected post-decode (compression_ratio LOO AUC ~0.85, full post-decode set ~0.99). The catastrophic regime (#21 compression_ratio ~18x) bounds P2: there the collapse is severe enough to distort even 1-dim text features (length). Net: dimensionality is the primary cause of the moderate asymmetry; a weak encoder-side no-speech signal adds marginal pre-decode predictability, but post-decode detection remains the deployable cure. Operational implication (consistent with #18): keep the text route on the reference-free ASR router (overlap/compression-ratio signals, post-decode), and always read emotion from the separated track. Pre-decode prevention of the attractor is at best weakly supported by this data; post-decode detection (#21's compression-ratio + token-id lock-in union) remains the deployable cure.
